programmes.show_description: That is all from the Cellar Club tonight and Caroline Munro is here to bring the night to a close.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: Tennis Court. 1984. Stars Peter Graves & Hannah Gordan. A couple move into a house they have inherited, but it soon becomes apparent that the old tennis court is far from normal.
the Monster. 2021. Documentary. Directed by Thomas Hamilton. Features Ron Perlman, Guillermo del Toro. A look at the life and legacy of Boris Karloff, and the history of the genre he personified.
An insightful documentary by Peter Williams about George Foreman, a man of great faith who was known as one of the best fighters who ever lived, who twice held the World Heavyweight title.
1936. Crime. A financier accepts a bet to spend a night in the Chamber of Horrors. Starring James Carew, Charles Oliver & Lucille Lisle.
Market Day/Minnow Trap/Lobster Boat. 1986. Jack Hargreaves goes shopping at a country market and catches small fish in the nearby stream, before heading out to sea on a lobster trawler.
1954. Crime. Director Terence Fisher and starring John Bentley. Reporters investigate threats against a retired army officer and discover links to a series of murders that occurred during the war.
Winner Take All. 1975. Stars Simon Cuff, Deddie Davies & John Sanderson. Peterkin deserts his master and runs right into the outlaws! Meanwhile, Sister Alice remains the outlaws' prisoner.
1997. Stars Brendan Pollecutt, Fezile Mpela & Robert Finlayson. As Dean and Thabo escape a tribal attack planned by the lord, they continue on their dangerous adventure.
Witches Brew. 1957. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. A woman with mystical powers convinces King Bors she is working evil. Merlin is enlisted against these powers.
The Wail of the Siren. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward, Yvonne Craig & Joan Collins. The Siren is launching a two part plan to become the richest and most wicked woman in Gotham City.
The Brothers O'Dell. 1955. Stars Roy Rogers, Trigger and Dale Evans.The O'Dell brothers are at odds when one's outlaw gang robs Mineral City's Wells Fargo office which is managed by the other.
White as Snow. 1967. Stars Donald Gray, Elizabeth Morgan & Ed Bishop. When the Mysterons threaten to kill Colonel White, he tries to leave Cloudbase so the other agents aren't in jeopardy.
The Shrew. 1959. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Hedda, lured away by a message that her sister is ill, finds herself an unwilling pawn in Gessler's latest plot.
Mr. Beamish and the Hangman's Noose. 1957. Stars Robert Shaw, Peter Hammond & Paul Hansard. A new governor arrives from England and arrests Beamish. A wary Tempest sets out to find answers.
The Wager. 1956. Starring: Alexander Gauge, Geoffrey Keen & George Rose. Friar Tuck and Robin bet to see who can raise more money. Robin will dress and act as a beggar.
1966. Sci-fi. Directed by Arthur C. Pierce. Stars Wendell Corey, John Agar & Keith Larson. When a starship becomes stranded on an unexplored planet, a mission is launched to save the survivors.
1942. War. Directed by John Farrow. Stars Anna Lee, Paul Muni & Ray Collins. A Norwegian fisherman, enraged at the Nazi atrocities against his village, sets out to seek revenge and restore peace.
Put your dancing shoes on and take a step back in time for Eden Kane's Someone Wants to Know.
The Remittance Man. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Tony Wickert & Stuart Wagstaff. A family travel to meet their son who was sent to the colonies years ago, but Chris Cobb discovers something shocking.
1962. Drama. Director: Lewis Gilbert. Stars Alec Guinness, Dirk Bogarde & Maurice Denham. During the French Revolutionary War, Captain Crawford is stuck in a battle of with his second in command.
1962. A look at the progress of the European Common Market during a time in which member countries have started to remove trade barriers.
The Fontenay Murders. 1963. Stars Rupert Davies, Edward Chapman & Alan Wheatley. Maigret discovers local prejudices run deep when investigating a series of murders in the town of Fontenay.
A Nice Class of Premises. 1976. Stars Gemma Jones, Donald Burton & Kate Lansbury. All bets are off when HRH becomes His Majesty, King Edward VII. Louisa and Augustus must plan for what lies ahead.
1979. Drama. Director: Harley Cokeliss. Stars Ray Winstone, Julie Shipley & Tony London. A teenager gets out of reform school and heads to Torquay for a seaside holiday with three friends.
1978. Drama. Director: Alan Rudolph. Stars: Geraldine Chaplin, Anthony Perkins & Moses Gunn. Just released from prison, a woman arrives in town to start a new life. Soon starting trouble.
1994. Hosted by Antoine de Caunes and Jean Paul Gaultier. Latino's do drag best, especially at this beauty contest in Brazil. Watch an interview with Thalia and Lorena, Mexico's top soap stars.
1956. Drama. Director: Pierre Gaspard-Huit. Stars Brigitte Bardot, Micheline Presle & Louis Jourdan. A country girl is discovered by a Paris magazine and becomes a top model.

The Beginning Part 1. 1973. Stars Keir Dullea, Gay Rowan & William Osler. Discovering a hidden door in the earth, three farmers break through it to find they are not on Earth, they are on a spaceship.
Flaming Havoc. 1941. Stars Warren Hull & Keye Luke. Staff  at 'The Sentinel' become suspicious that their temporary manager might be involved with a crime syndicate that is defrauding the lottery.
The Black Castle. 1956. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. Torwald holds Lord Trebizond to ransom in the Black Castle. Lorraine, rides to Camelot to seek help.
1965. Whatever Lola Wants. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. Honey is hired to escort a man to a charity ball, unaware that he is a murderer.
1960. Comedy. What a Whopper is a comedy from former documentarist Gilbert Gunn. Adam Faith stars in this tale about a struggling writer who fakes photographs of the Loch Ness monster.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Key of the Door
programmes.show_description: 1963. An intriguing look at the men and women of tomorrow who are needed to extend technology.
1957. Thriller. Directed by Ken Annakin. Stars Rod Steiger, David Knight & Bernard Lee. An unscrupulous financier absconds to Mexico in an attempt to avoid capture for embezzlement.
1963. Western. Director: Andrew V. McLaglen. Stars John Wayne, Maureen O'Hara & Patrick Wayne. Wealthy rancher McLintock uses his power and influence to keep the peace between conflicting groups.
The Critter Doctor. 1964. Stars Irene Ryan, Mark Goddard & Russell Collins. Granny gets frustrated when she confuses a house-to-house insecticide salesman with a critter doctor.
Flop Goes the Joker. 1967. Stars Adam, West Burt Ward & Alan Napier. The Joker makes his move against the Gotham City Museum for a grand extortion scheme.
1966. Fantasy. Directed by Jonathan Miller. Stars Anne-Marie Mallik, Peter Sellers & Wilfrid Brambell. Young girl Alice falls down a rabbit hole into a bizarre world full of eccentric characters.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life High and Dry
programmes.show_description: 1967. A compelling look at the coastal towns which make up the group of Cinque Ports along the south coast of England.
1985. The classic game show Give us a Clue hosted by Michael Parkinson, this week's guests are Judith Chalmers, Frazer Hines, Ian Ogilvy, Jack Watling, Lizzie Webb & June Whitfield.
The Deadly Decoy Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo and Kuryakin are told to escort a captured Thrush official to Washington.
The Footage Detectives are joined by stunt man extraordinaire Paul Weston, who has some brilliant stories on behind the scenes of Z-Cars Sergeant Cork and Gideons Way.
Double Jeopardy. 1989. Stars Robert Powell, Cathryn Harrison & Nigel Le Vaillant. When Hannay is entrusted with a dozen diamonds, he little knows the danger and distress they will bring.
A Holiday Romance. 1983. Stars Mollie Sugden, Christopher Blake & Jennifer Lonsdale. Ida meets a gentleman while on holiday with Angie and Robert. Robert takes an instant dislike to him.
Stranglehold. 1955. Stars John Bentley, Christopher Lee & Maureen Swanson. Peter Stanhope's ex-wife turns up after being presumed dead in the war. Betty and the butler plan a nasty blackmail.
The Case of the Great Pearl Robbery. 1964. Stars John Barrie, William Gaunt & Charles Morgan. Marriott investigates after a pearl necklace, worth £130,000, goes missing.
1972. With Freddie Starr and Peter Goodwright, guest acts such as Janet Brown, Johnny More and Paul Melba play a number of roles in a series of sketches and songs!
1967. Sci-Fi. Directed by Terence Fisher. Starring Christopher Lee, Peter Cushing & Patrick Allen. A heatwave forces the atmosphere to boil. It falls on Godfrey Hanson to discover the reason.

programmes.show_name: The Heritage Chart Show with Mike Read
programmes.show_description: A TOTP style TV Show for Heritage Artists based on the Heritage Chart which is voted on in over 80 countries. Songs you love from popular artists, live and on video with the odd interview.
